steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Use the triangle angle sum property
steps.content:
The sum of the three angles in any triangle is always $180^{\circ}$. Since we know $\angle X = 24^{\circ}$ and $\angle Y = 98^{\circ}$, we can write:
$$\angle X + \angle Y + \angle Z = 180^{\circ}$$

steps.title: Substitute known values and solve for ∠Z
steps.content:
Substitute the known angle measures:
$$24^{\circ} + 98^{\circ} + \angle Z = 180^{\circ}$$
Combine the known angles:
$$122^{\circ} + \angle Z = 180^{\circ}$$
Subtract $122^{\circ}$ from both sides:
$$\angle Z = 180^{\circ} - 122^{\circ} = 58^{\circ}$$
That is the correct answer.
